channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US-Iran deal allows Tehran to immediately resume oil exports, official says
item.description: A US official confirmed that under the memorandum of understanding reached with Iran, Tehran can immediately begin selling oil and fuel, marking a significant easing of sanctions that had constrained Iranian crude exports.

item.title: Bank of Japan raises rates to 31-year high, signals further hikes ahead
item.description: The Bank of Japan raised interest rates to their highest level in 31 years in a landmark policy normalization step, signaling readiness to tighten further as it focuses on taming price pressures from the Iran-war-induced energy shock.

item.title: Trump signals swift return of sanctions on Russian oil as G7 refocuses on Ukraine
item.description: President Trump and fellow G7 leaders moved to reimpose sanctions on Russian oil shipments, shifting focus back to the Ukraine war after the Iran deal dominated early summit discussions.

item.title: European leaders soften tone toward Trump at G7 after Iran deal breakthrough
item.description: A peace framework with Iran and hope for cooperation on Ukraine softened the tone at the G7 summit, with European leaders who had clashed with Trump now seeking common ground on trade and security.

item.title: Trump meets world leaders at G7 as Iran deal takes center stage
item.description: World leaders sought clarity on the Iran deal framework at the G7 summit, with Iran demanding Israel withdraw from Lebanon as part of the agreement, while Trump said Netanyahu needs to be "more responsible."

item.title: Israel Refuses Lebanon Withdrawal Despite Iran Deal Pressure
item.description: Israel says it will not withdraw forces from Lebanon even as Iran insists the US-Iran peace deal requires Israeli troops to leave. Hezbollah claims Iran has pledged to pursue Israeli withdrawal as a condition for any final nuclear agreement.

item.title: US Military Plans Permanent Weapons Stockpile in Australia
item.description: US Navy documents reveal advanced planning for a large war-ready weapons stockpile in Australia, with $30 million allocated to build warehouses and offices. The move signals deeper US military posture in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Ukrainian Su-24 Bomber Crashes, Both Pilots Killed
item.description: A Ukrainian Su-24 bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi Oblast on the evening of June 16, killing both the pilot and navigator. The Air Force confirmed the incident, with the cause under investigation.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strike halts operations at Moscow oil refinery
item.description: A Ukrainian drone attack sparked a fire at Moscows largest fuel-supplying refinery, halting operations. Zelenskiy described the strike as coercion to push Russia toward peace talks.

item.title: Trump signals swift return of sanctions on Russian oil as G7 refocuses on Ukraine
item.description: At the G7 summit in France, Trump indicated he may reimpose sanctions on Russian oil soon, as world leaders pushed Ukraine back to the top of the agenda alongside the Iran deal.

item.title: Trump insists Strait of Hormuz will open toll-free under Iran deal
item.description: Speaking at the G7 summit, Trump declared the Strait of Hormuz would reopen toll-free under the US-Iran deal, as oil prices fell 5% to a three-month low on hopes of restored shipping.

item.title: The Iran war permanently altered the global economy
item.description: The New York Times analyzes how the US-Iran conflict has fundamentally reshaped global trade, energy markets, and economic alliances in ways unlikely to revert even with a peace deal.

item.title: ECB will stay proactive on inflation even after Iran deal, Lane says
item.description: ECB chief economist Philip Lane said the central bank will remain proactive in fighting high inflation despite falling energy prices following the US-Iran deal, signaling rates may stay elevated.

item.title: G7 allies push Trump to reinstate sanctions on Russian oil as summit refocuses on Ukraine
item.description: U.S. allies at the G7 summit pressed President Trump to swiftly reimpose sanctions on Russian oil, shifting focus back to Ukraine amid signs the Iran war is de-escalating. The move signals continued Western resolve on isolating Moscow economically.

item.title: Trump says he will send Iran peace deal to Congress for approval
item.description: President Trump announced he would submit the U.S.-Iran war-ending agreement to Congress for approval, a key step that could determine whether the deal survives domestic political scrutiny on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Iran soccer team forced to train in Tijuana after being blocked from U.S. entry
item.description: Iran World Cup squad was denied entry to train in the United States amid wartime tensions, finding an unlikely welcome in the Mexican border city of Tijuana — a story where sport and geopolitics collide.

item.title: Trump criticizes Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks at G7, warns Israel to be more responsible
item.description: At the G7 summit, Trump publicly criticized Israeli PM Netanyahu for escalating attacks on Lebanon and Hezbollah, saying Israel must act more responsibly — a rare rift between the two leaders on full display before other world leaders.

item.title: Anti-capitalist protesters clash with security at G7 summit in Evian
item.description: Thousands of anti-capitalist demonstrators protested near the G7 summit venue in Evian-les-Bains, opposing what they called the elite global order — a familiar backdrop for the annual gathering of world leaders.

item.title: AI price war deepens across China as competition intensifies between tech giants
item.description: Narrowing capability gaps between AI models and falling service costs are fueling a price war among Chinese tech firms, with analysts predicting further reductions as competition heats up.

item.title: Harris says Trump DOJ investigation of Newsom fits pattern of targeting political enemies
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris condemned the Trump administration investigation into California Governor Gavin Newsom, calling it part of a broader pattern of using the Justice Department against political opponents.

item.title: Swiss luxury resort chosen as venue for US-Iran deal signing ceremony
item.description: The Bürgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne in Switzerland, long favored by world leaders and celebrities, will host the formal signing of the US-Iran peace agreement scheduled for June 19.

item.title: Philippines unprepared for a mega-quake despite Mindanao disaster, experts warn
item.description: A 50-year-old building code and stalled political action leave the Philippines vulnerable to a catastrophic earthquake, with scientists warning the recent Mindanao quake is only a preview of a larger event.

item.title: Trump tells G7 Ukraine war has "no impact" on the United States
item.description: At the G7 summit in Evian, President Trump distanced the US from the Ukraine conflict, telling allies the war has no effect on America — a stark signal to European partners who have relied on US security guarantees for decades.

item.title: Zelenskiy says G7 leaders agreed Russia is not winning, discussed further sanctions
item.description: Ukraine's president said G7 leaders reached consensus that Russia is not winning the war and discussed additional sanctions to push Moscow toward negotiations, after a closed-door session with Trump at the Evian summit.

item.title: Trump rebukes Israel at G7, suggests Syria should handle Hezbollah instead
item.description: President Trump publicly criticized Israel's prolonged and deadly campaign in Lebanon, saying the war is "too long" and suggesting Damascus — not the IDF — should deal with Hezbollah, a proposal that could reshape the Levant's security landscape.
